summary:
Radiation-induced meningiomas can be treated with stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) as it has been shown to achieve high rates of tumor control with low complications. Treatment suitability depends on factors like location near vital structures, tumor size, and vascular integrity due to associated morbidities. 

Note: This summary focuses only on clinically significant aspects related directly to efficacy and safety considerations specific to this patient population. It does not include off-topic details from general meningioma management or unrelated radiological findings.

Final Answer: Yes
